_meta.description: Lunar impact ratings for all 28 Arkansas sport fish species. Impact scale: HIGH > MED-HIGH > MEDIUM > LOW-MED > LOW.
sources: Quigley et al. — 'Popular solunar tables fail to predict fishing success in North American recreational freshwater trout fisheries' (peer-reviewed)
Fish'n Canada — 350,000 catch records over 40 years, walleye Full Moon correlation
Lukas & Sass 2011 — largemouth bass lunar activity telemetry study
AGFC Randy Zellers direct quote — bream full moon confirmation via Arkansas.com

species.name: White Bass
species.dayStrategy: Target baitfish schools in open water. Full Moon concentrates shad in coves — follow sonar marks toward shallow flats.
species.nightStrategy: Aggressive topwater bite under Full Moon. Cast around dock lights and bright shoreline areas. White or chrome lures.
species.source: Fish'n Canada 350,000-record study; AGFC White Bass management guide

species.name: Walleye
species.dayStrategy: Hunt deeper structure (10–20 ft) and channel edges during daylight. Tapetum lucidum eye gives dominant advantage after dark.
species.nightStrategy: Peak feeding under Full Moon. Slow jigging near bottom, or trolling cranks along main lake points and rock piles.
species.source: Fish'n Canada 40-year catch record study (350,000 records confirm Full Moon correlation)

species.name: Crappie
species.dayStrategy: Vertical jigging near brush piles and submerged timber. Pre/post-spawn bedding activity concentrated around Full Moon windows in spring.
species.nightStrategy: Suspend small jigs (1/32–1/16 oz) under dock lights. Full Moon triggers aggressive night bites near structure.
species.source: AGFC Crappie management; seasonal solunar correlation studies

species.name: Black Crappie
species.dayStrategy: Clearer water preference — target submerged grass edges and brush in 6–12 ft. Full Moon concentrates spawning in spring.
species.nightStrategy: Night biting under lights near Full Moon. Favors slightly deeper structure than White Crappie after dark.
species.source: AGFC Crappie management; species-specific solunar studies

species.name: White Crappie
species.dayStrategy: Tolerates turbidity — target stained-water brush piles and channel edges. Full Moon spawn timing applies in spring.
species.nightStrategy: Light-attracted baitfish draws crappie under Full Moon near dock and bridge lights.
species.source: AGFC Crappie management; solunar correlation studies

species.name: Striped Bass
species.dayStrategy: Follow shad schools on main lake humps and channel edges. Electronics key — mark bait, fish directly below.
species.nightStrategy: Surface-feeding blitzes on shad under Full Moon. White swimbaits or topwater poppers over open water.
species.tailwaterNote: Cannot reproduce in Arkansas — AGFC-stocked. Lunar influence is feeding-only, not spawn-driven.
species.source: AGFC Striped Bass stocking program; feeding behavior correlation studies

species.name: Hybrid Striped Bass
species.dayStrategy: Open-water shad schools; mid-column presentations on main lake points and river channels.
species.nightStrategy: Aggressive surface feeding under Full Moon near shad schools. Topwater or white swimbaits effective.
species.tailwaterNote: Cannot reproduce naturally — AGFC-stocked. Feeding-only lunar correlation.
species.source: AGFC Hybrid Striper stocking program; Striped Bass feeding analogue studies

species.name: Bluegill
species.dayStrategy: Shallow bedding areas in spring. Full Moon concentrates spawning activity May–July. Target gravel flats in 1–4 ft.
species.nightStrategy: Less aggressive after dark; focus on pre-dawn and dusk windows within 3 days of Full Moon.
species.source: AGFC Randy Zellers: 'focus on the days before and after the Full Moon' for bream bedding — Arkansas.com

species.name: Redear Sunfish
species.dayStrategy: Beds near mussel and snail habitat in sandy-bottomed coves. Full Moon triggers spawning beds through summer months.
species.nightStrategy: Not a strong night feeder. Prime windows are early morning within 2 days of Full Moon.
species.source: AGFC Redear Sunfish management; Full Moon bream bedding correlation (Arkansas.com)

species.name: Flathead Catfish
species.dayStrategy: Ambush spots near woody structure and undercut banks. Activity extends through dusk — Full Moon stretches feeding windows.
species.nightStrategy: Most active nocturnal predator. Full Moon periods produce consistent trophy-class catches on live bait.
species.source: Solunar theory (Knight 1936); AGFC Flathead Catfish management

species.name: Sauger
species.dayStrategy: Deep river channel structure and current breaks in daylight. Tapetum lucidum eye similar to Walleye — thrives after dark.
species.nightStrategy: Active night feeder. Full Moon amplifies feeding windows along main river channels and below dams.
species.source: AGFC Sauger management; walleye/sauger shared photoreceptor biology

species.name: Saugeye
species.dayStrategy: Structure-oriented in daylight — points, ledges, rocky transitions. Hybrid of walleye/sauger light-sensitive behavior.
species.nightStrategy: Full Moon feeding windows similar to walleye parent. Jig or live minnow along main lake structure.
species.source: AGFC Saugeye stocking program; hybrid walleye/sauger behavior studies

species.name: Largemouth Bass
species.dayStrategy: Cover-oriented (laydowns, docks, vegetation). Moderate lunar influence; spawn timing and temperature outweigh lunar cycles.
species.nightStrategy: Some topwater activity near Full Moon. Black or dark lures along weed edges and dock perimeters.
species.source: Lukas & Sass 2011 — telemetry study showing limited but present lunar correlation

species.name: Smallmouth Bass
species.dayStrategy: Current seams in rivers; rocky ledges and boulders in reservoirs. Moderate lunar sensitivity.
species.nightStrategy: Some night feeding near Full Moon along rocky structure. Tube baits and crayfish imitators.
species.source: Lukas & Sass 2011 framework; AGFC Smallmouth Bass management

species.name: Spotted Bass
species.dayStrategy: Rocky points, ledges, and mid-depth cover. Behaves similarly to Smallmouth with moderate lunar sensitivity.
species.nightStrategy: Moderate night activity near Full Moon. Finesse presentations along deep structure.
species.source: AGFC Spotted Bass management; bass lunar correlation studies

species.name: Kentucky Bass
species.dayStrategy: Submerged timber and rocky main-lake structure. Mid-range lunar sensitivity shared with other black bass.
species.nightStrategy: Limited night feeding; some activity during strong Full Moon near deep structure.
species.source: AGFC Kentucky Bass management; Lukas & Sass 2011 framework

species.name: Green Sunfish
species.dayStrategy: Shallow structure and bank cover. Moderate lunar correlation similar to other sunfish during spring spawn.
species.nightStrategy: Minimal night activity; focus on dawn/dusk windows near Full Moon during spawning season.
species.source: Solunar theory framework; AGFC sunfish management

species.name: Warmouth
species.dayStrategy: Heavy cover — woody debris, stumps, and aquatic vegetation. Moderate response to lunar cycles in spring.
species.nightStrategy: Some activity near Full Moon; better at dawn and dusk than true night.
species.source: Solunar theory framework; AGFC sunfish management

species.name: Channel Catfish
species.dayStrategy: Active throughout the day with moderate lunar influence on peak feeding windows. Channel breaks and deep holes.
species.nightStrategy: Night fishing effective year-round; modest Full Moon enhancement. Stinkbaits and cut bait.
species.source: Solunar theory framework; AGFC Channel Catfish management

species.name: Blue Catfish
species.dayStrategy: Main channel structure, ledges, and dam tailraces. Moderate lunar sensitivity — bait selection matters more.
species.nightStrategy: Good night feeder year-round; lunar impact is secondary to proper baiting and structure location.
species.source: Solunar theory framework; AGFC Blue Catfish management

species.name: Brown Trout
species.dayStrategy: Generation schedule dominates in tailwaters. Feed on invertebrate hatches (mayflies, midges) during low-generation windows.
species.nightStrategy: Large Brown Trout are nocturnal hunters; slight Full Moon advantage. Streamers after dark on low-generation nights.
species.tailwaterNote: Generation schedule overrides lunar influence in tailwaters. Check SWPA/USACE release schedule first.
species.source: Quigley et al. (peer-reviewed: solunar tables fail to predict CPUE in tailwater trout); AGFC Brown Trout management

species.name: Paddlefish
species.dayStrategy: Filter-feeders targeting zooplankton; some plankton migration is lunar-correlated. Snag fishing in deep, slow river bends.
species.nightStrategy: Minimal night-specific advantage. Snag fishing depends on fish congregation, not active feeding.
species.source: AGFC Paddlefish management; plankton tidal/lunar migration studies

species.name: Longnose Gar
species.dayStrategy: Ambush predators near shallow bays and slow backwaters. Slight spawning lunar correlation in May–June.
species.nightStrategy: Some activity in warm months near Full Moon. Rope lures for gar are daytime-focused.
species.source: AGFC Longnose Gar management; gar spawning biology studies

species.name: Alligator Gar
species.dayStrategy: Apex ambush predator in slow, warm backwaters. Spring spawn shows some lunar correlation. Target large structure.
species.nightStrategy: Occasional nocturnal hunting but not strongly lunar-driven. Primary feeding is opportunistic.
species.source: AGFC Alligator Gar management; gar spawning biology

species.name: Bowfin
species.dayStrategy: Spring spawn shows some lunar correlation; guards nests aggressively. Heavy cover — vegetation and woody debris.
species.nightStrategy: Can be active at night but not strongly lunar-dependent. Aggressive on artificial lures near spawning areas.
species.source: AGFC Bowfin management; primitive fish spawn biology

species.name: Rainbow Trout
species.dayStrategy: Generation schedule dominates in tailwaters. Focus on post-generation feeding windows when flows stabilize.
species.nightStrategy: Minimal lunar advantage. Tailwater flows and temperature determine feeding activity — check generation schedule.
species.tailwaterNote: Generation schedule overrides lunar influence. Quigley et al. confirmed solunar tables fail to predict CPUE in tailwater trout fisheries.
species.source: Quigley et al. peer-reviewed study; AGFC Rainbow Trout management

species.name: Cutthroat Trout
species.dayStrategy: Similar to Rainbow in tailwaters. Flow-driven activity dominates over lunar cycles. Dry flies during hatches.
species.nightStrategy: Minimal lunar-driven night advantage. Generation schedule is primary predictor of feeding activity.
species.tailwaterNote: Generation schedule overrides lunar influence in Arkansas tailwaters.
species.source: Quigley et al. peer-reviewed study; AGFC Cutthroat Trout management

species.name: Brook Trout
species.dayStrategy: Cold-water specialist in spring creeks and tailwaters. Feeding tied to generation and temperature more than lunar phase.
species.nightStrategy: Minimal lunar influence. Focus on generation release windows and temperature stability (below 65°F).
species.tailwaterNote: Generation schedule overrides lunar influence in Arkansas tailwaters.
species.source: Quigley et al. peer-reviewed study; AGFC Brook Trout management
